arts.ttl: Chelsea make winning start to Conference League
arts.sum: Chelsea made a winning debut in the third-tier Conference League as Renato Veiga, Pedro Neto, Christopher Nkunku and Kiernan Dewsbury-Hall all scored in a 4-2 win over Belgium's Gent at Stamford Bridge.

arts.ttl: Chelsea player ratings vs Gent as Veiga excellent, Neto shines and Felix magic
arts.sum: Chelsea shone in the second-half to secure three points in their UEFA Conference League league phase opener against Gent at Stamford Bridge. And the so-called 'B team' made the perfect start with Renato Veiga heading the ball in following a fine cross from Mykhailo Mudryk from the right-hand side.

arts.ttl: Maresca praises Chelsea – but still wants improvement
arts.sum: Chelsea reaction. The post Maresca praises Chelsea – but still wants improvement appeared first on West London Sport.

arts.ttl: Chelsea next five fixtures compared to Arsenal, Aston Villa and Man City after Gent win
arts.sum: The Premier League match against Chelsea at Anfield is followed by a visit to RB Leipzig in the Champions League, and then a trip to take on Arsenal. There has even been talk round whether Enzo Maresca's side can challenge Man City, Arsenal and Liverpool for the Premier League title.

arts.ttl: Maresca's Dewsbury-Hall moment, Disasi thanked and things spotted in Chelsea win
arts.sum: The Chelsea boss was to make one more for Thursday's game against Gent as he rotated all 11 players and handed opportunities to players such as Veiga, Dewsbury-Hall and Cesare Casadei. Regular minutes in the head coach's first-choice Blues XI have been incredibly hard to come by, with the midfielder even left out of the squad for the win over Brighton due to the amount of players available to Maresca right now.

arts.ttl: Italy talent Casadei ‘forcing himself’ into conversation for Chelsea starts
arts.sum: Italy Under-21 starlet Cesare Casadei again impressed when playing for Chelsea in the Conference League...

arts.ttl: 'He was fantastic': Peter Crouch blown away £12m Chelsea player after his display in the win over Gent
arts.sum: Peter Crouch singled out one Chelsea player for big praise after Thursday night’s win in Europe against Gent. Chelsea are up and running in the...

arts.ttl: Lee Carsley wants Jadon Sancho to find a ‘home’ before talk of England return
arts.sum: The winger has made a bright start to his loan spell at Chelsea, while Carsley revealed Harry Maguire still has a role to play in his England team

arts.ttl: Enzo Maresca was absolutely thrilled when ‘amazing’ Chelsea player scored vs Gent – journalist

arts.ttl: Second-string Chelsea side ease to comfortable win over Gent
arts.sum: Renato Veiga, Pedro Neto, Christopher Nkunku and Kiernan Dewsbury-Hall were on target for Chelsea.
